signals.signal_description: Devon-Coterra merger completed May 7, 2026. Combined $58B enterprise value, 1.6M BOED production, HQ Houston. $1B annual pre-tax synergy target by YE2027 — includes capital optimization, operating margin improvements and IT systems consolidation. Integration Management Office established. Trey Lowe leads technology for combined entity.

signals.signal_title: AI, automation and RPA confirmed as Devon core competencies
signals.signal_description: Devon LinkedIn profile explicitly names Artificial Intelligence, Intelligent Automation, and RPA as core organizational capabilities. Databricks + Azure + SAP HANA confirmed tech stack. 22% faster drilling, 19% faster completions in 2025. Simulfrac >70% of completions in 2026.

Q1 2026: 22% faster drilling times, 19% faster completions vs 2024. Simulfrac >70% of completions in 2026. $4.8B liquidity. Net debt/EBITDAX 0.9x. Combined entity operational performance tracking ahead of plan.

enrichment_notes: Premier shale E&P, significantly larger post-Coterra merger (completed May 7, 2026). HQ relocated from Oklahoma City to Houston (STOLA, channel_mismatch_flag now false). Combined $58B enterprise value. $1B annual synergy target by YE2027 — IT systems consolidation is explicit component. Integration Management Office established. CTO Trey Lowe confirmed in combined executive team reporting to CEO Clay Gaspar. Devon standalone FY2025 revenue ~$16B, EBITDA ~$5.8B, ~4,500 employees (post-merger combined entity significantly larger). Databricks + Azure + SAP HANA confirmed. AI/automation/RPA named as core competencies. 22% faster drilling 2025. Simulfrac >70% completions 2026. ms_standardized=false — no M365 evidence. ai_maturity=13 — approaching ≥15 with Coterra AI capabilities. MASSIVE WWT opportunity: active IT integration of two large E&P companies, $1B synergy target requires systems consolidation, Houston HQ now in-territory, Trey Lowe is the named technology entry point.
